name: optillm
tagline: An OpenAI-compatible proxy that boosts reasoning accuracy at inference time
seoTitle: OptiLLM - Optimizing Inference Proxy for LLMs
metaDescription: OptiLLM is an OpenAI API-compatible inference proxy that applies 20+ reasoning techniques at request time to improve LLM accuracy on math, coding, and logic.
overview: OptiLLM is an OpenAI API-compatible inference proxy that sits between your application and your language model. Instead of changing the model itself, it spends extra compute at request time, running techniques like Mixture of Agents, planning search, and multi-agent reasoning to improve answers on math, coding, and logic tasks.

It works as a drop-in replacement for any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. You point your existing OpenAI client at the local OptiLLM server and pick a technique by adding a prefix to the model name (for example 'moa-gpt-4o-mini'). Through LiteLLM it can route to OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Cerebras, and many other models, so no fine-tuning or training is needed.

As an LLM gateway, OptiLLM fits teams that already call a chat-completions API and want better reasoning results without swapping models or retraining. You keep your client code and just route calls through the proxy.
features: OpenAI API-compatible: works as a drop-in proxy for any OpenAI-compatible client or endpoint
Over 20 inference-time optimization techniques, from best-of-N to MCTS, planning search, and multi-agent reasoning (MARS, CePO, MOA)
Pick a technique per request by adding a prefix to the model name (for example 'moa-' for Mixture of Agents)
Multi-provider routing via LiteLLM, covering OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Cerebras, and many other models
No model training or fine-tuning required, the improvements come purely from extra compute at inference
SSL options for self-signed certificates and corporate proxies via --ssl-cert-path or OPTILLM_SSL_CERT_PATH

gettingStarted.intro: Install OptiLLM, start the proxy server, then point any OpenAI client at it and choose a technique through the model name prefix.

steps.heading: Install
steps.body: Install the package from PyPI. A Docker image is also available at ghcr.io/algorithmicsuperintelligence/optillm.

steps.heading: Start the server
steps.body: Set your API key and run the optillm command. The server listens on port 8000 by default.

steps.heading: Call it from an OpenAI client
steps.body: Point the OpenAI client at the local server and add a technique prefix (such as 'moa-') to the model name.
steps.code:
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(base_url="http://localhost:8000/v1")

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="moa-gpt-4o-mini",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Solve: If 2x + 3 = 7, what is x?"}]
)

useCases: Improving accuracy on math, coding, or logic prompts without switching to a larger model or fine-tuning
Adding inference-time reasoning techniques to an existing app that already uses an OpenAI-compatible client
Routing requests across OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and other providers through one proxy via LiteLLM
Running self-hosted via Docker behind a corporate proxy with custom CA certificates
